label: Episodic memory

node.label: Episodic memory
node.definition: Past interactions or events are stored as retrievable experiences with temporal context.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Episodic memory is modeled as depending on Sequences, events & time series, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Conversational assistants & chatbots, Autonomous software-engineering agents, Browser, desktop & computer-use agents.
examples: atlas downstream link to Conversational assistants & chatbots
atlas downstream link to Autonomous software-engineering agents
atlas downstream link to Browser, desktop & computer-use agents
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Sequences, events & time series, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: Episodic memory stores, selects, or obtains information through Sequences, events & time series.
Episodic memory stores, selects, or obtains information through Embeddings & distributed representations.
In this atlas graph, Episodic memory has a `uses` dependency on External retrieval & ranking because External retrieval & ranking is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
